a:r.a:t: Letting me stop without losing my matches is the single most important design decision in this whole flow.
“Not a fit” bucket: the single best sentence on the site
Path A / Path B split kept her in the funnel
“On treatment, looking for better” option felt validating
DNAvisit appears unannounced at consent; would freeze
No side-effect or treatment-burden info on cards
No cost or insurance information on cards
Visual surface “competent but a little cold”
Headline copy “the linguistic equivalent of beige”
Doctor is missing as a third-party stakeholder
Brings a binder; forgets questions at appointments
Wants to print matches and bring them in
Treats MG decisions as collaborative, not unilateral
Chirpy or marketing-y first follow-up email
“We miss you” or generic re-engagement patterns
Generic green-healthcare imagery and stock photos
Hiding the business model (who actually pays?)
Anonymized patient stories with no human face
Blurred match cards that feel like withholding
